scenario: A FEEL expression must remove an element by position from a list: tags = ["A", "B", "C"] → remove position 2 → ["A", "C"]. (Position 2 is "B" since FEEL is 1-indexed.)
question: Which FEEL built-in fits?

options.text: splice(2, 1) -- this is a JavaScript Array method that removes elements from an array in place, using zero-based indexing. FEEL does not support the JavaScript dot-method calling syntax, the splice function name, or zero-based list indexing. FEEL lists are 1-indexed, and all list operations use the function call syntax function(arguments) without object method notation. Attempting to use splice in a FEEL expression would result in a parse error or unresolved name error at evaluation time.

delete(tags, 2) -- delete is an invented function name with no entry in the FEEL built-in function library. Modellers familiar with SQL (DELETE statements), Python (del keyword), or JavaScript object operations sometimes guess this name for element removal. The correct FEEL function for removing an element by position from a list is remove(list, position), which uses 1-based indexing. Calling an undefined function in FEEL results in a null value or evaluation error depending on the FEEL engine's error handling.

2] -- this syntax attempts to use a ! " This notation is invented and not part of the FEEL specification. FEEL list indexing supports positive integers (tags[1] returns the first element), negative integers for counting from the end (tags[-1] returns the last element), and filter expressions using predicates. There is no ! negate index operator. To remove elements by position, FEEL provides the remove(list, position) function.

remove(tags, 2) -- FEEL's dedicated built-in function for removing an element by position from a list. The function takes two arguments: the list and the 1-based position index to remove. It returns a new immutable list containing all elements except the one at the specified position, so remove(["A", "B", "C"], 2) returns ["A", "C"]. FEEL lists are always 1-indexed (position 1 is the first element), and remove respects this convention. The original list is unmodified; FEEL list operations always produce new values rather than mutating existing ones.

a.text: Incorrect. remove(list, position) is FEEL's element-removal-by-position function. 1-indexed. Returns a new list (immutable lists). Use this when you know the position; for removal by value, FEEL has different patterns (filter / [item in list : item != target]).

b.text: Incorrect. Method-call syntax with .splice() isn't FEEL.

c.text: Incorrect. Not a FEEL function. Sometimes JS / Python users guess names.

d.text: Correct. FEEL list indexing supports positive integers (1-based) and negative integers (counting from end), but no ! negate-index.

explanation: FEEL list indexing supports positive integers (1-based) and negative integers (counting from end), but no ! negate-index.
